title: Daily Market Digest: June 8, 2026
subtitle: A professional overview of today's market activities, without specific investment advice.

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rebound as Chip Sector Leads Recovery; Geopolitical Tensions and Inflationary Concerns Persist
items.summary: U.S. equity markets saw a notable rebound on Monday, with the Nasdaq leading the charge, as technology and semiconductor stocks recovered from last week's sharp selloff. Investors closely monitored ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which continued to influence oil prices, and considered the implications of recent economic data on Federal Reserve policy. Market sentiment remained cautiously optimistic amidst inflation worries and the prospect of further interest rate adjustments.

items.statContext: +392 points, surging on chip stock recovery

items.headline: Marvell Technology ($MRVL)
items.summary: Shares jumped following the announcement that the company will join the S&P 500 on June 22nd. This move, coupled with positive comments from NVIDIA's CEO, fueled significant investor interest in the AI chipmaker.

items.headline: Micron Technology ($MU)
items.summary: The semiconductor giant rebounded sharply, recovering from a significant decline on Friday, as AI-linked stocks saw renewed buying interest.

items.headline: Nurix Therapeutics ($NRIX)
items.summary: Experienced a substantial gain after Roche agreed to pay the biopharmaceutical company up to $2.3 billion for rights to an experimental blood-cancer drug.

items.headline: Wix.com ($WIX)
items.summary: Fell significantly after the web-platform company announced an expected $50 million reduction in bookings for 2026, attributed to a new organizational realignment and a slowdown in its Partners business.

items.headline: Perusahaan Perseroan (Persero) PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia Tbk ($TLK)
items.summary: Was among the biggest pre-market losers, with specific reasons for the decline not immediately detailed in available reports.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Sectors Lead Market Gains; Financials and Real Estate Lag
items.summary: Technology, particularly AI-linked semiconductor stocks, drove Monday's market recovery, benefiting from strong demand in AI infrastructure buildout. The energy sector also performed well as crude oil prices rose amidst Middle East tensions. Conversely, financial services and real estate sectors remain undervalued, with financials being the worst-performing sector year-to-date due to overestimation of net interest income growth.

items.headline: May Jobs Report Fuels Fed Rate Hike Expectations; CPI and PPI Ahead This Week
items.summary: The stronger-than-expected May jobs report, showing 172,000 nonfarm payroll additions and a steady 4.3% unemployment rate, has intensified expectations for a Federal Reserve rate hike. Markets are now pricing in a 71-72% probability of a December rate increase. Investors are keenly awaiting the May Consumer Price Index (CPI) on Wednesday and the Producer Price Index (PPI) on Thursday, both of which are anticipated to reflect elevated inflationary pressures.

items.headline: Middle East Tensions Elevate Oil Prices Amid Israel-Iran Exchange
items.summary: Geopolitical developments in the Middle East, marked by an exchange of strikes between Israel and Iran, continued to weigh on global markets. Crude oil prices, including Brent crude, rose above $97 per barrel due to renewed hostilities, contributing to broader inflationary concerns. While a ceasefire was urged, the ongoing situation introduces volatility.

items.headline: Campbell's Reports Q3 Results; Oracle and Adobe Earnings on Deck
items.summary: Campbell's ($CPB) reported its third-quarter fiscal 2026 results today, with net sales decreasing 4% and adjusted EPS falling 32% to $0.50, though the company reaffirmed its full-year guidance. Looking ahead, major earnings reports from Oracle ($ORCL) are expected on Wednesday evening, followed by Adobe ($ADBE) on Thursday.
